stemHtml: A pilot is planning a VFR flight which crosses a controlled airspace. Because of restrictions of the controlled airspace, part of the flight must be flown at a low level. Which of the following is a problem of navigation at low level that could be faced in a VFR flight?

options.a: Visual range to the horizon is restricted at low level.
options.b: Features with great vertical extent are harder to see.
options.c: The feeling that you are flying more slowly over the ground changes how you perceive distance.
options.d: Navigation features can be noticed more easily, but are more difficult to identify.
